name: source_transient_memory_contract
description: Source documents are transient: read once, extract, write to wiki — then FORGET. Full source content never persists in agent memory beyond the ingest turn where it was processed.

core_principle.statement: Source document contents (raw/ files, web_search results, PDF text) exist only in the immediate processing context of a single ingest step. They are NOT carried forward to subsequent steps or preserved across turns.
core_principle.rationale: Every token holding raw source content competes with wiki page writing and crosslinking — wasting limited context window on data already captured into wiki pages.
core_principle.best_practice_reference: Based on context engineering principles: 'tool-result clearing' (Anthropic), 'context as finite budget'

phase_extract.action: write extracted knowledge to wiki page (new or existing)
phase_extract.constraint: only structured facts go into wiki — not raw source text verbatim

phase_forget.trigger: after phase_extract completes AND wiki page is written to disk
phase_forget.action: EXPLICITLY discard full source content from active context
phase_forget.instruction_template: Source document [{SOURCE_TITLE}] processed. Knowledge captured in wiki page [{WIKI_PAGE_PATH}]. Discard full source text — it is now re-fetchable from disk via read_file if needed later.

forbidden_pattern.description: Keeping source content after wiki page write
forbidden_pattern.example: After writing page, still referencing 'the document says X...' in subsequent steps
forbidden_pattern.why_wrong: Source content is re-fetchable from disk; holding it wastes context budget

batch_ingest_contract.problem: When ≥3 related sources are ingested together, each source's full content stays in context until ingest completes — exponential memory usage.
rule: Process ONE source at a time through the read → extract → forget cycle
Before reading next source: EXPLICITLY discard previous source's raw content from working context
Only accumulated wiki pages + metadata persist between sources

prohibited_pattern.description: Reading all N sources into context, then processing them together
prohibited_pattern.why_wrong: N×document_size tokens in context simultaneously — fastest way to overflow
prohibited_pattern.alternative: Read source A → extract → forget A → read source B → extract → forget B → ...

explicit_sequence: 1. Read source A → extract entities/facts → write wiki page for A
2. FORGET: Discard source A content from context; keep only path + title
3. Read source B → extract → write wiki page (or append to existing)
4. FORGET: Discard source B content from context

generated_wiki_pages_lifecycle.description: After new wiki pages are created during ingest, their full content should also be discarded once validated.

lifecycle.action: write new/updated wiki page to disk

lifecycle.instruction_template: Wiki page [{WIKI_PAGE_PATH}] created/updated and validated. Content written to disk with proper structure and crosslinks. Discard full page content from context — it is retrievable via read_file at any time.

exception_cases.description: e.g., step_3 analysis needs source text for contradiction check — allowed, but must be scoped to that step only. After the step completes, forget.
exception_cases.forget_trigger: immediately after the specific sub-step that needed it

exception_cases.description: Direct quotes from source → extracted and written into wiki page during phase_extract. After writing, quote is no longer in raw form in context.
exception_cases.forget_trigger: after the quote has been embedded in the wiki page content

mental_model: Treat each source document like a PDF reader: open, highlight key points, close, forget.
The wiki page IS the memory — not your conversation context.
If you need to re-read something from a source, grep the wiki (it's indexed), don't re-read the raw source.
Context window is a budget: every token holding stale content degrades performance on current task.

practical_actions.immediate_next_step: write them into wiki page frontmatter/content
practical_actions.follow_up: EXPLICITLY forget source content before moving to next step

practical_actions.action: complete wiki write for this source
practical_actions.follow_up: EXPLICITLY forget page content after validation passes

explicit_forget_phrases_to_use: Source [{TITLE}] processed and forgotten. Knowledge captured in wiki [{PATH}].
Discarding source content — it is now re-fetchable from disk.
Wiki page validated and written. Content discarded from context.

integration_with_existing_architecture.working_memory: sources are tracked in WM.sources_ingested list (paths only, no content)
integration_with_existing_architecture.log_md: append source path and wiki page created — not full summary of source
integration_with_existing_architecture.raw_registry_json: stores hash + title + metadata — never stores raw content
